2014-08-01.title: U.S. Seeks Release of Couple in Qatar, Creating Tensions With a Crucial Arab Ally
2014-08-01.abstract: The State Department asked Qatar to lift a travel ban against an American couple being held in connection with the death of their adopted daughter.

2014-08-01.title: Ancient Haven for Refugees Sees Signs of Strain
2014-08-01.abstract: Struggling to accommodate the thousands fleeing civil war in Syria, the Turkish government has quietly begun moving them to camps outside of the cities.

2014-08-01.title: How Many Ebola Patients Have Been Treated Outside of Africa?
2014-08-01.abstract: Questions and answers on the scale of the outbreak and the science of the Ebola virus.

2014-08-01.title: African Leaders Coming to Talk Business May Also Be Pressed on Rights
2014-08-01.abstract: The Obama administration said it would seek to build deeper relationships with African leaders, though some advocates worry that human-rights issues will take a back seat.

2014-08-01.title: School Shelters in Gaza Offer Limited Safety, and Even Less Comfort
2014-08-01.abstract: Life for about 200,000 people packed into more than 80 schools serving as shelters in Gaza is full of deprivation and discomfort.

2014-08-02.title: A Test for Ukraine in a City Retaken From Rebels
2014-08-02.abstract: In Slovyansk, a city of 80,000 that was a stronghold for pro-Russian rebels, Ukraine authorities have a difficult task ahead to shift the loyalties of residents to keep the peace.

2014-08-02.title: Britain Fails to Find Riches It Expected in Swiss Accounts
2014-08-02.abstract: When the British tax authorities struck a landmark deal with the Swiss to crack down on tax evasion, they sat back and waited for the cash to flow in. Almost three years later, they are still waiting.

2014-08-02.title: Anti-Semitism Rises in Europe Amid Israel-Gaza Conflict
2014-08-02.abstract: Attacks, threats and hate speech have yielded alarm among many Jews and stern language from government leaders.

2014-08-02.title: Birthright Trips to Israel Continue Despite Mideast Conflict
2014-08-02.abstract: There have been some cancellations since the hostilities began, but 6,000 young people have visited Israel as planned.

2014-08-02.title: North Korea’s Antipathy for the U.S. Is Seen at a New High
2014-08-02.abstract: North Korea’s deputy permanent representative, Ri Tong-il, recited grievances with the United States and South Korea, accusing them of conspiring for regime change in the North.

2014-08-03.title: Hospitals in Gaza Overwhelmed as Attacks Continue
2014-08-03.abstract: More than 120 Palestinians were killed in Rafah, Gaza Strip, on Friday and Saturday. Those deaths, and hundreds of injuries, overwhelmed the city’s health care facilities.

2014-08-03.title: Airstrike Near U.N. School Kills 10 as Israel Shifts Troops in Gaza
2014-08-03.abstract: As Israel began to redeploy significant numbers of troops away from populated areas of Gaza on Sunday, an Israeli Air Force missile struck near the school entrance.

2014-08-03.title: 1914: Germany Officially Declares War on France
2014-08-03.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: Germany declared war on France in 1914.

2014-08-03.title: 1939: Woman M.P. Is 1st to Wed in Commons Crypt
2014-08-03.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: The first wedding of a woman Member of Parliament at Westminster Crypt, in 1939.

2014-08-03.title: China Says Nearly 100 Are Killed in Week of Unrest in Xinjiang
2014-08-03.abstract: The Chinese authorities have characterized the unrest as terrorism, but Uighur advocacy groups have blamed a sweeping security crackdown.

2014-08-04.title: Nepal Enthralled by Visit of India Premier, Who Hits ‘the Right Notes’
2014-08-04.abstract: A fractious country, Nepal has been united in its embrace of Narendra Modi, the first Indian prime minister in 17 years to make a state visit here.

2014-08-04.title: To Evade Russians, Plane Crossed Into Sweden
2014-08-04.abstract: An American reconnaissance plane crossed into Swedish air space last month as it sought to avoid being intercepted by Russian fighters, American military officials said.

2014-08-04.title: European Sanctions Force Russian Carrier to Suspend Flights
2014-08-04.abstract: Dobrolet, a low-cost subsidiary of the Russian state carrier Aeroflot, announced that it would suspend all flights as a result of sanctions imposed on Russia by the European Union.

2014-08-04.title: A City Divided Over an Occupation Without End
2014-08-04.abstract: To some, the barricades in Kiev are an essential reminder of the price Ukrainians paid in blood to protect their freedom, but others want them gone.

2014-08-04.title: Separatist Cadre Hopes for a Reprise in Ukraine
2014-08-04.abstract: The pro-Russian leadership in eastern Ukraine is pressing ahead with a sweeping goal of establishing a lasting government.

2014-08-05.title: Visit From Indian Premier Signals a Political Shift in Nepal
2014-08-05.abstract: Prime Minister Narendra Modi appealed to both politicians and devout Hindus during a recent visit as he announced plans to increase ties between the countries.

2014-08-05.title: Ukraine Rebels Conscript ‘Punishment Brigades’ for Support Work
2014-08-05.abstract: Glum-looking detainees, often stripped to their underwear, can be seen around Donetsk digging ditches, clearing brush and peeling potatoes.

2014-08-05.title: Buildup Makes Russia Battle-Ready for Ukraine
2014-08-05.abstract: Adding battalions near the border with Ukraine could allow Russia to launch a cross-border incursion there with little or no warning, Western officials said.

2014-08-05.title: Lax Quarantine Undercuts Ebola Fight in Africa
2014-08-05.abstract: Sierra Leone, the nation with the most cases of the disease, has banned many public gatherings and set strict quarantine rules, but health officials are deeply worried by loose enforcement efforts.

2014-08-05.title: Israel Suspends Attack in Parts of Gaza
2014-08-05.abstract: Minutes after a partial cease-fire was to begin, the Israeli air force struck a refugee camp in Gaza City, killing an 8-year-old and wounding at least 29 others.

2014-08-06.title: Vatican Ends Suspension of Nicaraguan Priest
2014-08-06.abstract: The Vatican has issued a decree lifting Rev. Miguel F. d’Escoto Brockmann’s suspension after nearly 30 years.

2014-08-06.title: Arrest by Israel in Abduction of 3 Youths Is Made Public
2014-08-06.abstract: Israel arrested a Palestinian man last month accused of being the prime mover in the kidnapping and murder of three Israeli teenagers in June, it emerged from court papers.

2014-08-06.title: Putin Urges Economic Retaliation for Sanctions Over Ukraine Conflict
2014-08-06.abstract: The Russian president said that the measures were necessary to protect manufacturers of consumer goods in his country.

2014-08-06.title: A Part of Hamas Negotiates, and Another May Fight Again
2014-08-06.abstract: As Israel and the Palestinians prepared for talks to extend a truce, a senior Hamas leader said the group would keep its political and military wings separate, allowing the latter to prepare for another potential war.

2014-08-06.title: Catalan Vote Seen as Test for Separatists in Europe
2014-08-06.abstract: As Catalonia prepares to hold a referendum on independence this fall, concern is growing that the vote could set off a separatist spiral in Spain and other independence-minded regions in Europe.

2014-08-07.title: Lebanon: Border Standoff Eases
2014-08-07.abstract: The clashes between the Lebanese Army and Sunni extremist fighters from the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria, who occupied a Lebanese border town last weekend, showed some signs of easing on Wednesday.

2014-08-07.title: Islamic Militants in Iraq Are Widely Loathed, Yet Action to Curb Them Is Elusive
2014-08-07.abstract: Any action against the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria is seen as having unintended and potentially harmful consequences.

2014-08-07.title: Sunni Extremists Repel Kurdish Forces in Iraq
2014-08-07.abstract: The Islamic State in Iraq and Syria appeared to be making a push along the Kurdish border to take ground, control oil fields and water and expel minority groups.

2014-08-07.title: Israeli Premier Voices Regret for Civilian Casualties, but Blames Hamas
2014-08-07.abstract: “Every civilian casualty is a tragedy, a tragedy of Hamas’s own making,” Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said.

2014-08-07.title: Conflict Leaves Industry in Ashes and Gaza Reeling From Economic Toll
2014-08-07.abstract: In addition to the estimated 1,800 Palestinians killed, business leaders say some 175 industrial plants took devastating blows.

2014-08-08.title: Key Cleric in Egypt Rejects Executions
2014-08-08.abstract: In a rare rebuke, Egypt’s highest Muslim religious authority refused to approve court-ordered death sentences on leaders of the Muslim Brotherhood, court officials said.

2014-08-08.title: In Peru, Trading Boats for Boards
2014-08-08.abstract: On Peru’s northern coastline, the long history of reedboats is threatened as a new generation looks beyond fishing for careers and opportunities like surfing.

2014-08-08.title: Uighurs’ Veils Signal Protest Against China’s Restrictions
2014-08-08.abstract: The Chinese authorities have cast a net over a wide range of Uighur practices, including veils and long beards, seen as signs of religious extremism.

2014-08-08.title: Israeli Procedure Reignites Old Debate
2014-08-08.abstract: The Israeli military’s “Hannibal procedure” is intended to prevent captors from getting away with their captives, even at the risk of endangering the lives of the captured Israeli soldiers.

2014-08-08.title: Town’s Floating Symbol Fading Into the Sunset
2014-08-08.abstract: Small boats made of reeds have been used for hundreds of years in a Peruvian village, but the reed beds are dwindling and so is interest in the fishing life the boats make possible.

2014-08-09.title: Half a Ton of This, Half a Ton of That: Russia Is Calm Over Food Import Bans
2014-08-09.abstract: Retaliatory sanctions against Western products have led to a “buy Russian” campaign and some signs that there were ways around the problem.

2014-08-09.title: Motorbikers in North Korea Are Told to Park to Save Fuel
2014-08-09.abstract: Motorbike use by ordinary citizens has been cut to rush hours to ensure fuel for the government and the military personnel, two websites that monitor North Korea said, adding that a decline in Chinese oil imports was probably responsible.

2014-08-09.title: Turkey Votes for a President
2014-08-09.abstract: Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan Erdogan is considered the leading candidate as Turkey conducts its first direct election for president.

2014-08-09.title: Turkey’s Premier Tries to Keep Power, as President
2014-08-09.abstract: Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan is expected to win Sunday’s presidential vote and transform an office that has been largely ceremonial.

2014-08-09.title: Differences Between Israel and U.N. Over Gaza Episodes Go Back Years
2014-08-09.abstract: Israeli attacks on United Nations facilities are repeating a pattern that an agency board of inquiry reported in 2009. Israel says it is addressing the issues.

2014-08-10.title: 44 Dead in Tour Bus Crash in Tibet
2014-08-10.abstract: A bus carrying about 50 passengers crashed into two vehicles and fell 30 feet off a mountainous highway on Saturday, the state news media said.

2014-08-10.title: Kerry Urges Myanmar to Stay on Path to Democracy, Despite Stumbles
2014-08-10.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry, on the final day of a trip to Myanmar, pushed for further progress in what many perceive to be slowing political and legal overhauls.

2014-08-10.title: Agreeing to More Talks in Egypt, Israelis and Palestinians Begin Latest Cease-Fire
2014-08-10.abstract: The new truce began at 12:01 a.m. Monday, after both sides agreed to a resumption of Egyptian-mediated negotiations toward a more durable solution.

2014-08-10.title: Capitalizing on U.S. Bombing, Kurds Retake Iraqi Towns
2014-08-10.abstract: As Kurdish officials said they were making advances against Sunni militants, Iraq's political crisis deepened, with a deadline expiring for the president to choose a nominee for prime minister.

2014-08-10.title: 1939: U.S. Zionist Delegation to Geneva in Quest of Solution to Palestine
2014-08-10.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: An American delegation travels to the World Zionist Congress in 1939.

2014-08-11.title: Taliban Take Responsibility for Bombing in Kabul
2014-08-11.abstract: While they have repeatedly fired rockets at the airport and carried out a broad campaign of violence across the country, the suicide attacks that have been a hallmark of the war have been less common in the capital.

2014-08-11.title: Ebola May Pose Little Threat to U.S., but It Looms Large on Twitter
2014-08-11.abstract: It is the vividness of a perceived threat, rather than the threat’s true potency, that plays the bigger role in the potential to spread through social media, researchers find.

2014-08-11.title: Scrambling Down an Iraqi Mountain, Yazidi Families Search for Missing
2014-08-11.abstract: Many Yazidis who had fled the advance of ISIS militants have become separated from their families, some in their flight to the mountains, and some when they made the decision to come down.

2014-08-11.title: Renewed Shelling in Donetsk
2014-08-11.abstract: Artillery pounded Donetsk, Ukraine, on Sunday as the West warned Russia that any attempt to send “humanitarian” troops into the region would be deemed “unacceptable.”

2014-08-11.title: In Gaza, Grief, Anger — and No Small Measure of Pride
2014-08-11.abstract: In interviews across Gaza, many Palestinians insisted they could withstand much more to achieve their goals of opening borders and removing Israeli restrictions on imports and exports.

2014-08-12.title: Killings Rise in Karachi as Taliban Target Police
2014-08-12.abstract: In the Pashtun slums of Karachi, Pakistan, militants have gunned down police officers, assaulted poorly defended police stations and dispatched suicide bombers to assassinate top police commanders.

2014-08-12.title: Standing Guard Over a Monarchy’s Tainted Legacy
2014-08-12.abstract: The guards at the massive mausoleums commemorating Italy’s former monarchs struggle to preserve respect for those who once united the country.

2014-08-12.title: For 2 U.S. Presidents, Iraqi Leader Proved a Source of Frustration
2014-08-12.abstract: Nuri Kamal al-Maliki rose from obscurity to power in part with American help, but U.S. leaders found him an often unconstructive ally who caused as many headaches as he solved.

2014-08-12.title: At Heart of Ebola Outbreak, a Village Frozen by Fear and Death
2014-08-12.abstract: More than 60 people have died from the virus in a tiny village in Sierra Leone, and now people there worry that a quarantine will cut off their food and supplies.

2014-08-12.title: Pentagon Says Airstrikes Have Slowed but Not Stopped Sunni Militants
2014-08-12.abstract: Airstrikes have slowed the progress of Sunni militants laying siege to refugees on Mount Sinjar, but Pentagon officials also said that ISIS remained on the offense.

2014-08-13.title: Why Obama Is Helping the Yazidis in Iraq
2014-08-13.abstract: With humanitarian crises around the world, a look at why President Obama decided to use military force to help the Yazidis, a religious minority in Iraq.

2014-08-13.title: In Increase, U.S. to Send 130 Advisers to Aid Iraqis
2014-08-13.abstract: The additional advisers will aid in the rescue of displaced people trapped by jihadists on Mount Sinjar, Defense Department officials said.

2014-08-13.title: In Pope’s Trip to South Korea, Church Envisions Growth
2014-08-13.abstract: During the first visit by a pontiff since 1989, Pope Francis will preach to laid-off workers and the country will witness a beatification.

2014-08-13.title: Indian Premier Denounces Pakistan for ‘Proxy War’
2014-08-13.abstract: On a visit to Kashmir, Narendra Modi condemned his neighbor as cease-fire violations rise in the border area.

2014-08-13.title: Using a Tactic Unseen in a Century, Countries Cordon Off Ebola-Racked Areas
2014-08-13.abstract: A “cordon sanitaire,” a tactic more common in the medieval era, is being carried out in Guinea, Sierra Leone and Liberia, but it has the potential to become inhumane.

2014-08-14.title: Plane Crash Kills Brazilian Politician
2014-08-14.abstract: Television footage of the immediate aftermath of a plane crash in a residential area that killed the Brazilian presidential candidate Eduardo Campos and six others.

2014-08-14.abstract: Eduardo Campos’s unexpected death leaves his supporters up for grabs for the leading candidates, as well as his running mate, who may take his place.

2014-08-14.title: Rocket Fire Casts Doubt on Truce in Gaza
2014-08-14.abstract: The new exchanges between Israel and elements in Gaza marred an agreement to extend a cease-fire for another five days.

2014-08-14.title: Saudis Give $100 Million to U.N. Fight on Terrorism
2014-08-14.abstract: A large gift to the United Nations Counter-Terrorism Center as Islamist militant extremism hits ever closer to home.

2014-08-14.title: For U.N. Leader on Human Rights, Finish Line Looks Blurry
2014-08-14.abstract: Known for her bluntness, Navi Pillay held the post of United Nations high commissioner for human rights longer than any of her predecessors.

2014-08-15.title: Maliki Steps Aside in Iraq
2014-08-15.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki of Iraq agreed to end his legal challenge to the nomination of his replacement, Haider al-Abadi.

2014-08-15.title: In Nepal, a Better Life With a Steep Price
2014-08-15.abstract: Plagued by endemic poverty, many young people in Nepal leave to work abroad. But the backbreaking labor they find there often results in death.

2014-08-15.title: A Ukrainian City Under Siege
2014-08-15.abstract: Luhansk is a grim place, but for residents, shelling remains the greatest danger and the most trying aspect of daily life.

2014-08-15.title: A Ukraine City Under Siege, ‘Just Terrified of the Bombing’
2014-08-15.abstract: Life in Luhansk, Ukraine, is tough, but far from catastrophic. The only relief most people here seek is from the shelling, which goes on night and day.

2014-08-15.title: U.S. Pulls Back on Plans for a Mountain Rescue
2014-08-15.abstract: Far fewer refugees were discovered on a mountain in Iraq, a U.S. team reported, and they were no longer either starving or stranded.

2014-08-16.title: Witness in War Crimes Court Tallies Cost of a Decade in Hiding
2014-08-16.abstract: When a young woman from Belgrade volunteered to testify in a case involving Slobodan Milosevic, she was promised protection. Today she says her life is ruined.

2014-08-16.abstract: Spanish analysts believe that the influx of Africans to Spain’s borders was orchestrated by its neighbor across the Strait of Gibraltar.

2014-08-16.abstract: Nearly a week after American airstrikes paved the way for Kurdish forces to reclaim Mahmour from the Sunni militants of ISIS, very few people have returned.

2014-08-16.abstract: Mr. Richards was a sergeant who was demoted after video footage surfaced of him and his colleagues urinating on corpses of Taliban fighters in Afghanistan.

2014-08-16.title: As World Boils, Fingers Point Obama’s Way
2014-08-16.abstract: Buffeted by international instability, the president stresses that there are limits to American power, and that the chief executive is not omnipotent.

2014-08-17.title: Battling Ebola in Liberia
2014-08-17.abstract: Efforts to identify and quarantine people infected with Ebola in the poor West Point neighborhood of Monrovia have been haphazard, chaotic and largely ineffective.

2014-08-17.abstract: Facing the prospect of impossibly long waiting lists for organs, some patients have turned to an underground trade that pairs them with people willing to sell an organ.

2014-08-17.abstract: Amos Canaf, founder of an advocacy group for renal patients, argues that the kidney shortage leaves patients with few choices other than to go overseas for a transplant.

2014-08-17.title: Advocate for Deceased Organ Donation
2014-08-17.abstract: Rabbi Shlomo Amar believes that recovering an organ from someone who is brain dead does not violate Jewish law.

2014-08-17.title: Opposes Deceased Organ Donations
2014-08-17.abstract: Rabbi Yeshayahu Heber set up an organization to promote living donation amongst Orthodox Jews but believes that organ donation by the brain dead violates Jewish law.

2014-08-18.title: Case in Which Abortion Was Denied Reignites Controversy Over Irish Law
2014-08-18.abstract: The woman, who is not an Irish citizen, sought an abortion under a clause in the new Protection of Life During Pregnancy Act, saying that she was suicidal.

2014-08-18.title: The Chasm Tilting Scots Toward Independence
2014-08-18.abstract: Conservatives’ sway has dwindled in the north, and some left-leaning Scots are thinking of going their own way.

2014-08-18.title: The Displaced in Gaza
2014-08-18.abstract: With 11,000 homes destroyed and many more damaged, a humanitarian crisis is just beginning.

2014-08-18.title: In Torn Gaza, if Roof Stands, It’s Now Home
2014-08-18.abstract: With an estimated 11,000 homes destroyed and many more severely damaged, Gaza’s housing and humanitarian troubles are just beginning.

2014-08-18.title: For Chinese, Pope Seems Worlds Away in South Korea
2014-08-18.abstract: The sparse news treatment of the pope’s first visit to the Far East is indicative of the Chinese Communist Party’s continuing attempts to maintain a tight grip on Catholicism.

2014-08-19.title: Attacks on Aid Workers Jump Worldwide, Group Says
2014-08-19.abstract: The sharp rise over the past year and a half reflects a surge in conflict zones.

2014-08-19.title: Airstrikes Over Tripoli Kill Six; Source of Bombing Is Unknown
2014-08-19.abstract: The warplanes struck targets controlled by Islamists, and Libyan authorities attributed the attacks to a foreign state.

2014-08-19.title: Surviving Ebola, but Untouchable Back Home
2014-08-19.abstract: As some survivors come back to the world of the living, they feel a chill of wariness, or worse, from neighbors.

2014-08-19.title: Amid Election Impasse, Calls in Afghanistan for an Interim Government
2014-08-19.abstract: Several top officials in the government say a ruling committee might be best for now as the candidates in Afghanistan’s recent presidential vote dispute the results.

2014-08-19.title: In China, Myths of Social Cohesion
2014-08-19.abstract: China’s Communist Party has devoted enormous resources to composing historical narratives, like that of the Fragrant Concubine, that seek to legitimize its rule.

2014-08-20.title: Saudi Prince Is Identified as Target of Paris Heist
2014-08-20.abstract: While the police in France have refused to release his identity, the French news media reported that it was Prince Abdul Aziz, 41, who was ambushed by gunmen in a Hollywood-style robbery.

2014-08-20.abstract: In what separatist officials call the “Ukrainianization” of the leadership, almost all the original Russian leaders of the rebellion have resigned and gone home, replaced by Ukrainians of dubious qualifications.

2014-08-20.title: A Trove of Diaries Meant to Be Read by Others
2014-08-20.abstract: Having collected more than 7,000 personal memoirs, Italy’s Pieve Santo Stefano has become known as the City of Diaries.

2014-08-20.title: German Leader Emerges as Key Figure in Ukraine Talks
2014-08-20.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany is emerging more and more as a pivotal leader on the Ukraine conflict as she dances delicately between opposing forces.

2014-08-20.title: Russia, Iran and Egypt Heckle U.S. About Tactics in Ferguson
2014-08-20.abstract: Strident criticism of the action taken by the police in Ferguson, Mo., has come from authoritarian nations where the police are often venerated and dissent is scarcely tolerated.

2014-08-21.title: Rifts in a Crimean Art Colony
2014-08-21.abstract: The Crimean town of Koktebel was a tolerant oasis for artists and writers in the midst of Soviet repression. Now, its residents are divided by the Russian annexation of Crimea.

2014-08-21.abstract: The light and landscape of coastal Koktebel has drawn artists for nearly a decade, but Russia’s annexation of Crimea has split the normally peaceful oasis into feuding factions.

2014-08-21.title: Ebola and the Problem of Panic
2014-08-21.abstract: Unrest in a slum in Monrovia, Liberia, is a case study for the panic and challenges doctors and patients face in poor, densely populated urban areas.

2014-08-21.title: A New Entry Is Shaking Up Brazil’s Vote
2014-08-21.abstract: Marina Silva is emerging as one of the most disruptive figures in Brazilian politics in a generation, with a chance to be elected president.

2014-08-21.title: From Missouri to Syria, Journalists Are Becoming Targets
2014-08-21.abstract: Reporters, editors and those who monitor the freedom of the press describe a harsh environment for reporters, in the United States and abroad.

2014-08-22.title: German Broadcaster Fires Chinese Blogger
2014-08-22.abstract: Many commentators on Chinese-language social media see hidden motives in the firing of Su Yutong, a prolific blogger on Deutsche Welle’s Chinese-language website.

2014-08-22.title: In Steps of Grandfather, a German Soldier in France
2014-08-22.abstract: A reporter’s grandfather was among the multitudes of German soldiers who stormed into France and Belgium and left some 27,000 French soldiers dead 100 years ago.

2014-08-22.title: Foreign Minister Ahmet Davutoglu Nominated to Be Turkey’s Next Premier
2014-08-22.abstract: Turkey’s governing party nominated Ahmet Davutoglu to succeed President-elect Recep Tayyip Erdogan as prime minister and party chairman.

2014-08-22.title: Share Your Family’s World War I History
2014-08-22.abstract: The New York Times invites you to share your family’s story from World War I, and describe efforts you have made to learn about how that war affected your family.

2014-08-22.title: Telling Blows Against Hamas
2014-08-22.abstract: Israel killed three top commanders of Hamas’s armed wing in predawn airstrikes that also took other Palestinian lives.

2014-08-23.title: Two New Cases of Ebola Stem From Secondhand Contact
2014-08-23.abstract: Although the cases in Nigeria involve the spouses of health workers who had cared for the Liberian-American who carried the virus, the country says its containment strategy is working.

2014-08-23.title: U.S. Officials and Experts at Odds on Threat Posed by ISIS
2014-08-23.abstract: With the rapid advance of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria, the rhetoric the Obama administration is using to describe the danger the group poses to the United States has escalated.

2014-08-23.title: Gaza Talks Build at U.N.
2014-08-23.abstract: Diplomats said Germany, France and Britain were involved in discussions about a possible Security Council resolution.

2014-08-23.title: Blackouts in Egypt Prompt Accusations
2014-08-23.abstract: Officials blamed supporters of a deposed leader, while others pointed fingers back at the government.

2014-08-23.title: American’s Star Power Unrivaled in Japan
2014-08-23.abstract: David Spector, with his bleach-blond hair and ability to deliver one-liners in flawless Japanese, has been a fixture in Japan’s often raucous talk-show world for 30 years.

2014-08-24.title: China Says 8 Executed in Western Region; Charges Stem From Separatist Attacks
2014-08-24.abstract: The executions were the latest display of might by the Chinese government, which is trying to extinguish increasingly violent discontent among Uighurs.

2014-08-24.title: 1889: Sarah Bernhardt’s Courage in Real Death Scene
2014-08-24.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: The actress Sarah Bernhardt faces the funeral of her husband in 1889.

2014-08-24.title: In Eastern Ukraine, Rebel Mockery Amid Independence Celebration
2014-08-24.abstract: As Ukrainians celebrated their country’s independence, pro-Russian separatists in Donetsk staged a counter-parade to delight in the death and imprisonment of Ukrainian soldiers.

2014-08-24.title: Strife in Libya Could Presage Long Civil War
2014-08-24.abstract: Libya is being torn apart by an escalating war among its patchwork of rival cities and tribes, and an assault that destroyed its main airport last month has exacerbated the nation’s divisions.

2014-08-24.title: Ukraine Leader, Set to Meet Putin for Talks, Warns of Long Struggle
2014-08-24.abstract: As Ukraine celebrated its independence Sunday, President Petro O. Poroshenko, who is to meet with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia in Belarus on Tuesday for peace talks, left little doubt that he saw Moscow as the source of the conflict.

2014-08-25.title: Teenager Cites Ordeal as Captive of Israelis
2014-08-25.abstract: A 17-year-old Palestinian said Israeli soldiers detained him for five days, forcing him to search and dig for Hamas tunnels.

2014-08-25.title: Fishermen Cross an Imperceptible Line Into Enemy Waters
2014-08-25.abstract: Several thousand fishermen, both Pakistani and Indian, have been arrested at sea in recent years, accused of crossing a border they cannot see and whose exact location is in dispute.

2014-08-25.title: Israel Says Missile Strike Killed Hamas Official Handling ‘Terror Funds’
2014-08-25.abstract: The Israeli military said the Hamas official had been responsible for the militant group’s financial transactions.

2014-08-25.title: Britain Appeals to Anti-Extremist Imams in Effort to Uproot Seeds of Radicalization
2014-08-25.abstract: Britain has turned to the imams for help preventing British Muslims from adopting radical views, and for persuading militants to moderate their beliefs.

2014-08-25.title: ISIS Tightens Its Grip With Seizure of Air Base in Syria
2014-08-25.abstract: The fall of the Tabqa air base gave the militants effective control of Raqqa Province and highlighted the lack of local military forces that can effectively battle the group.

2014-08-26.title: Liberian Doctor Treated With an Experimental Drug Dies From Ebola
2014-08-26.abstract: Dr. Abraham Borbor, deputy chief medical doctor at Liberia’s largest hospital, received ZMapp — the same drug given to two infected Americans who survived.

2014-08-26.title: U.S. Says It Told Qatar Not to Pay a Ransom
2014-08-26.abstract: The Obama administration has insisted that it told the government of Qatar not to pay a ransom for the release of the captured journalist Peter Theo Curtis.

2014-08-26.title: Ex-Diplomat for the Vatican Could Be Tried
2014-08-26.abstract: The former ambassador to the Dominican Republic is accused of paying boys there to engage in sexual acts, and his diplomatic immunity has been revoked.

2014-08-26.title: Poland Embraces Football That Has a Rougher Side
2014-08-26.abstract: The most popular Polish sports — like volleyball — tend to avoid physical contact, but the Warsaw Eagles are cultivating a crowd that enjoys the rougher stuff.

2014-08-26.title: In India, an App for Chats and for Keeping Secrets
2014-08-26.abstract: The Hike instant messaging system lets users filter information to cloak aspects of their social lives from their parents.

2014-08-27.title: A Banker’s Bold Vision Inspires an Arts Center, and Neighbors’ Doubts
2014-08-27.abstract: The Centro Botín, designed by Renzo Piano, is rising in the northern city of Santander, along with complaints that public space is being exploited.

2014-08-27.title: Hard-Line Splinter Group, Galvanized by ISIS, Emerges From Pakistani Taliban
2014-08-27.abstract: The new group, known as Jamaat-e-Ahrar, is composed of disaffected Taliban factions from four of the seven tribal districts along the Pakistan border with Afghanistan.

2014-08-27.title: U.S. Fires on an Iranian Boat
2014-08-27.abstract: A United States Coast Guard vessel fired Tuesday on an Iranian boat in the Persian Gulf in what the Defense Department said was a defensive maneuver.

2014-08-27.title: Turkey: Chinese Workers Abducted
2014-08-27.abstract: Kurdish separatists kidnapped three Chinese engineers in an area near a power plant in Turkey’s southeast, a regional government official said Tuesday.

2014-08-27.title: U.S. Mobilizes Allies to Widen Assault on ISIS
2014-08-27.abstract: President Obama is nearing a decision to authorize airstrikes and airdrops around Amerli, an Iraqi town that has been under siege by militants for more than two months.

2014-08-28.title: Proposing New Capital Is Old Idea in Argentina
2014-08-28.abstract: President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner’s suggestion to consider making Santiago del Estero the nation’s capital reflects longstanding tension between Buenos Aires and Argentina’s provinces.

2014-08-28.title: From a Compound in the Brazilian Hills, Excellence in Math Emerges
2014-08-28.abstract: The National Institute for Pure and Applied Mathematics in Rio is gaining attention as a hidden gem in a country with few world-renowned educational institutions.

2014-08-28.title: Military Skill and Terrorist Technique Fuel Success of ISIS
2014-08-28.abstract: As fighters continue to seize territory, the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria has built an effective management structure of mostly middle-aged Iraqis, including many military officers under Saddam Hussein.

2014-08-28.title: Skirmishes Put Feeling of Wartime on India-Pakistan Border
2014-08-28.abstract: Nightly artillery firing across the border intensified in mid-August and reportedly has killed two Indian civilians and four Pakistani civilians.

2014-08-28.title: Iran Altering Reactor in Bid for Nuclear Deal
2014-08-28.abstract: The nation’s top nuclear official said the reactor would be redesigned to limit the amount of plutonium it could produce.

2014-08-29.title: Theme Park Offers Respite for War-Weary Iraqis
2014-08-29.abstract: Most people at Happy Park in Iraqi Kurdistan — many from wealthy families, or longtime residents seeking a break — were glad to have any kind of escape from ISIS militants.

2014-08-29.title: With Gaza War, Movement to Boycott Israel Gains Momentum in Europe
2014-08-29.abstract: The war in Gaza and its aftermath have inflamed opinion in Europe, analysts say, and are likely to increase support for campaigns to divest from and penalize Israel.

2014-08-29.title: An Israeli App Tracking the Gaza Conflict Has Followers Near and Far
2014-08-29.abstract: More than one million people have downloaded Red Alert, an app tracking rocket fire from Gaza that alerts users with a siren similar to those heard in the south of Israel.

2014-08-29.title: U.S. Identifies Citizens Joining Rebels in Syria, Including ISIS
2014-08-29.abstract: Nearly a dozen Americans are known to have traveled to Syria to fight for the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria, the militant group that the Obama administration says poses the greatest threat to the United States since Al Qaeda before the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.

2014-08-29.title: Air Encounter More Likely to Cause Alarm Than Change
2014-08-29.abstract: The Pentagon called a Chinese fighter pilot’s approaching an American spy plane “very dangerous,” but the Beijing-Washington division on surveillance flights will probably remain.

2014-08-30.title: Quarantine for Ebola Lifted in Liberia Slum
2014-08-30.abstract: The president of Liberia had rejected the advice of Ebola experts in quarantining the West Point neighborhood, a move that resulted in deadly clashes.

2014-08-30.title: Iraqi Kurds Expand Autonomy as ISIS Reorders the Landscape
2014-08-30.abstract: The ISIS invasion has physically cut off most of Kurdistan from the rest of Shiite-dominated Iraq and encouraged the Iraqi Kurds in their drive for expanded autonomy.

2014-08-30.title: Leadership and Calm Are Urged in Ebola Outbreak
2014-08-30.abstract: Leadership and help from Western nations are needed to eradicate the virus in West Africa, according to some of the top experts in disease eradication.

2014-08-30.title: U.S. Colleges See Little Risk From Ebola, but Depend on Students to Speak Up
2014-08-30.abstract: Universities and colleges in the United States also admit that they have limited ability to track students’ travels or their health.

2014-08-30.title: 50 Days of War Leave Israelis and Palestinians Only More Entrenched
2014-08-30.abstract: Both sides claimed victory, but the cease-fire is uneasy. Israeli citizens feel more insecure than before, and Hamas failed to win the concessions it demanded.

2014-08-31.title: U.S. Strikes Militants Besieging Turkmen in Iraq
2014-08-31.abstract: American planes struck the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria, and dropped food, water and aid near the northern town of Amerli.

2014-08-31.title: China Restricts Voting Reforms for Hong Kong
2014-08-31.abstract: China’s legislature laid down strict limits to proposed voting reforms in Hong Kong, drawing battle lines in what could be a deepening confrontation over the future of the city and of China.

2014-08-31.title: 1939: Britain on War Basis With Reservists Called, Navy Fully Mobilized, Telephone Connection Cut
2014-08-31.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: Britain starts preparing for war in 1939.

2014-08-31.title: Pakistani Army Calls for Calm After Protest Turns Deadly
2014-08-31.abstract: At least three people died in clashes with the police when demonstrators, calling for Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif’s resignation, marched toward his official residence.

2014-08-31.title: Affiliate of Al Qaeda Confirms Capture of U.N. Peacekeepers in Syria
2014-08-31.abstract: The Nusra Front, the Syrian branch of Al Qaeda, said it had seized more than 40 peacekeepers because the United Nations failed to help the people of Syria during the country’s civil war.
